Document [19] (Title: Pharmacogenomics Overview -- Clinical Significance -- Genes and Broad-Spectrum Drug Approvals) Other classes of drugs still have the potential to join the list of tumor-agnostic approvals. Isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) 1 and IDH2 mutations are common in cancers such as gallbladder cancer, gliomas, and acute myeloid leukemia, leading to the accumulation of 2-HG, which drives tumorigenesis. Targeted inhibitors, such as ivosidenib and enasidenib, reduce 2-HG levels, thereby restoring normal function. Vorasidenib, a newly FDA-approved IDH inhibitor, shows benefits for some low-grade gliomas in the human brain. Olutasidenib, a selective IDH1 inhibitor, has demonstrated strong remission rates in patients with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia, resulting in FDA approval. As research advances, IDH-targeted therapies continue to show promise in improving patient outcomes. [60] [ National Cancer Institute. Vorasidenib Treatment Shows Promise for Some Low-Grade Gliomas ]

Document [30] (Title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 214) The community describes blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) as a group of leukemic disorders comprising lymphoid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم النقوي), which correspond to chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), respectively. Targeted therapy relies heavily on protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, imatinib mesylát, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—block the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase; the former four are used for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors ibrutinib and zanubrutinib also belong to this class, with ibrutinib approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein kinase inhibitors include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as other ATC‑listed agents cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are employed in acute myeloid leukemia. Corticosteroids such as dexamethasone acetate (used for blood cancer and septic shock), dexamethasone phosphate, prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate demonstrate efficacy in hematologic malignancies. Chemotherapeutic agents irinotecan hydrochloride and pipobroman are also utilized. Contraindicated interventions include infection with human herpesvirus 1 and oncolytic viral therapy talimogene laherparepvec, which may exacerbate the disease. Collectively, these agents illustrate the role of kinase‑targeted and immunomodulatory drugs in the comprehensive management of leukemic disorders.
